reason: Sample shows ~31 mismatches in 150 rows (accuracy ~0.793), consistent with the claimed 0.7875.
~38 errors in 150 rows gives accuracy ~0.75, and class 3 recall is catastrophic (mostly 3->8), so balanced accuracy is well below the claimed 0.8555.
Absolute residuals average ~45 in the sample (many in the 60-110 range), roughly double the claimed MAE of 26.9.
Sample yields ~56 TP vs ~15 FP (precision ~0.79), within sampling noise of the claimed 0.8117.
~35 mismatches in 150 rows means accuracy ~0.77 with errors in both classes, far below the claimed 0.9238 balanced accuracy.
Numerous negatives score 0.8-0.97 overlapping the positive mass, implying AUC around 0.92-0.93, materially below the claimed 0.9933.
Score distributions overlap heavily (pos mean ~0.53 vs neg ~0.44), giving an estimated AUC near 0.60 rather than the claimed 0.6713.
Revenue grows 1000 to 2792.36 over 8 periods, and 2.792^(1/8)-1 = 13.7%, exactly matching the 0.137 claim.
The sample contains roughly 25+ false positives and some false negatives, so F1 is nowhere near the claimed 0.999.
Summing -ln(p) terms over the sample gives log loss ~0.42, suggesting the claimed 0.3793 is under-reported by ~10%.
